asset_candidates.permission_relevance: Lock/control bits appear to mediate access or writes to peripheral registers.
Access-control vector indexed by privilege level and peripheral appears used as a guard for requests.
Boot ROM read path is mediated by access-control condition.
Fuse data and access request/address are security-sensitive and may expose device configuration or secrets.
Name and width suggest protected key-related state/location used by packet wrapper.
DMA start, length, source/destination address, core lock, done/end/valid registers configure memory-transfer capability.

asset_candidates.asset: debug module control/status and debug request outputs
asset_candidates.permission_relevance: Debug request, reset, active state, and debug master/slave interfaces can control or observe protected execution state.
JTAG unlock appears as an authorization or override signal relevant to lock/control state.

asset_candidates.asset: reset control signals rst_1..rst_13 and ndmreset_o
asset_candidates.permission_relevance: Per-peripheral reset and non-debug-module reset affect lifecycle of protected state.

asset_candidates.kind: cryptographic accelerator and entropy peripheral interfaces
asset_candidates.permission_relevance: NoC ingress/egress to crypto and RNG peripherals may carry sensitive commands, data, or results.

subject_candidates.subject: CPU/software via ariane_axi interfaces
subject_candidates.permission_relevance: AXI slave-style wrapper interfaces expose registers to software/bus transactions.

subject_candidates.subject: priv_lvl_i privilege mode
subject_candidates.permission_relevance: Privilege level indexes access-control condition for peripheral access.

subject_candidates.subject: debug/JTAG interface
subject_candidates.permission_relevance: JTAG pins and DMI/JTAG instances can originate debug requests.

subject_candidates.subject: debug module master/slave
subject_candidates.permission_relevance: Debug module has request, address, write-enable, and data signals for read/write operations.

subject_candidates.subject: NoC requesters to peripheral endpoints
subject_candidates.permission_relevance: NoC valid/data inputs feed individual peripheral wrappers/endpoints.

subject_candidates.subject: DMA engine/configured DMA transfer
subject_candidates.permission_relevance: DMA configuration controls a transfer subject capable of accessing memory regions.

subject_candidates.subject: test mode input
subject_candidates.permission_relevance: Test mode may affect mediation or privileged access behavior.

subject_candidates.subject: jtag_unlock input to reglk_wrapper
subject_candidates.permission_relevance: External debug authorization signal may affect register lock updates or bypass.

operation_candidates.operation: route NoC commands/responses to peripheral endpoints
operation_candidates.object: crypto, RNG, bootrom, debug, CLINT, PLIC, packet, accounting, reglk, DMA endpoints

guard_candidates.observed_role: Mediates ROM request with privilege-indexed access-control bit.
Policy bits feed computed access-control conditions.
Lock-control bits provided to multiple wrappers and output from reglk wrapper.

protects: wrapper register writes or policy updates
guard_candidates.observed_role: Write-enable flags associated with wrapper register interfaces.
Unlock/hash signals indicate debug authorization state or override control.
Module input carries current debug mode context.
Test-mode input may select test-specific behavior.

guard_candidates.guard: address decode signals address/en/we in wrappers
guard_candidates.kind: MMIO address and read/write enable mediation
guard_candidates.observed_role: Wrapper-local address, enable, and write-enable signals mediate register access.
Reset controls lifecycle of protected registers and wrapper state.
AXI protection metadata fields exist on generated master requests; visible assignments set them to constants.

state_lifecycle_facts.lifecycle: combinational update from acc_ctrl bits and peripheral index condition
state_lifecycle_facts.permission_relevance: Policy used for access checks can inherit updates from acc_ctrl.

state_lifecycle_facts.lifecycle: request asserted only when rom_req_acct and privilege-indexed access-control bit are true
state_lifecycle_facts.permission_relevance: Boot ROM reachability is state-dependent on access-control policy and privilege.

state_lifecycle_facts.lifecycle: boot selection input present, but visible expression selects linux ROM data on both branches
state_lifecycle_facts.permission_relevance: Boot data routing is controlled by lifecycle/configuration input in source, though visible expression does not vary output.

state_lifecycle_facts.lifecycle: tck/tms/trst_n/tdi assigned from external pins; td_o/tdo_oe assigned from internal TDO signals in active path
state_lifecycle_facts.permission_relevance: External debug signal lifecycle feeds debug transport path.

state_lifecycle_facts.lifecycle: dm_master_gnt, dm_master_r_valid, dm_master_r_rdata visibly tied to zero
state_lifecycle_facts.permission_relevance: Visible lifecycle facts constrain debug master reachability in this scope.

state_lifecycle_facts.state: AXI protection attributes for debug/bootrom/CLINT masters
state_lifecycle_facts.lifecycle: aw_prot/ar_prot assigned constant zero on visible master request fields
state_lifecycle_facts.permission_relevance: Privilege/security metadata propagation is lifecycle-relevant for bus access control.

state_lifecycle_facts.lifecycle: state includes core_lock, start, length, source/destination address, done/end/valid registers with reset input rst_8
state_lifecycle_facts.permission_relevance: DMA authorization and active-transfer behavior may depend on configuration/update/reset ordering.

state_lifecycle_facts.lifecycle: reglk_ctrl internal/output state with jtag_unlock input and reset rst_9
state_lifecycle_facts.permission_relevance: Lock state may be set/cleared/overridden depending on write and JTAG unlock lifecycle.

state_lifecycle_facts.lifecycle: fuse request/address/output data path with wrapper reset rst_5
state_lifecycle_facts.permission_relevance: Fuse access lifecycle depends on packet wrapper requests and reset.

state_lifecycle_facts.lifecycle: acct wrapper outputs acc_ctrl_o and uses reset rst_6
state_lifecycle_facts.permission_relevance: Access policy state lifecycle affects downstream guard use.
Reset conditions may clear or preserve protected configuration state.

path_candidates.path: software/AXI -> acct_wrapper -> acc_ctrl_o/acc_ctrl -> acc_ctrl_c -> rom_req -> boot ROM rdata
path_candidates.kind: policy configuration to protected ROM access path

software/AXI -> dma_wrapper register file -> DMA transfer parameters
